questions.question: What does {1, 2, 2, 3} evaluate to?
questions.explanation: Sets automatically remove duplicates, so {1, 2, 2, 3} becomes {1, 2, 3}.

questions.question: How do you create an empty set?
questions.explanation: {} creates an empty dictionary. Use set() to create an empty set.

questions.question: What is the result of {1, 2, 3} & {2, 3, 4}?
questions.explanation: The & operator finds the intersection — items present in both sets. Only 2 and 3 are in both.

questions.question: What is the difference between remove() and discard() on a set?
options: remove() raises an error if the item is missing; discard() does not
discard() raises an error if the item is missing; remove() does not
remove() removes all items; discard() removes one
questions.explanation: remove() raises a KeyError if the item is not found, while discard() silently does nothing.

questions.question: Complete: {1, 2, 3} ___ {3, 4, 5} would give the result {1, 2} (items in first but not second).
Which of the following is TRUE about frozenset?
options: It can be used as a dictionary key
questions.explanation: A frozenset is immutable and hashable, so it can be used as a dictionary key — unlike a regular set.
